rulings.Case Title: TATIANA SANOCHKINA et al VS. MATHEW HUI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT, ETC.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 12, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT MABEL TENG'S MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT, ETC. This court denies defendant Mabel Teng's Reply In Support of Motion for Determination of Good Faith of Settlement. It appears that plaintiffs received nothing in return for the dismissal of their action against defendant Teng in exchange for her promising to not collect from defendant Yuen's arbitration award. Such a lack of consideration was dispositive in Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.App.3d 488, 496. ("P]laintiffs received nothing in return for the dismissal of their action . . . except relief from having to pay [the settling defendant's] costs because they were wrongfully sued . . . To say that section 877.6 cloaks [the settling defendant] with immunity from liability to joint tortfeasors under these circumstances would . . . frustrate the goal of allocating costs equitably among multiple tortfeasors." Plaintiff's settlement lacks consideration, could make defendant Huey solely liable to plaintiffs, and would bar him from indemnifying defendant Teng for any compensatory or punitive damages from the alleged structural and mold damage. This court grants judicial notice of Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: FIA CARD SERVICES N.A. VS. CHARLENE CUN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 12, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF FIA CARD SERVICES N.A.'S Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. Defendant's claim of exemption from wage garnishment is granted. Defendant's financial statement demonstrates that the difference between her monthly income and monthly expenses is insubstantial and therefore her wages are exempt. rulings.Case Title: LESLIE FINLEY VS. DREXEL A. BRADSHAW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Defendant Bradshaw And Associates Pcs Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendants Request For Production Of Documents Set Two To Defendants Special Interrogatories Set Two And To Compel Production Of Documents From Plaintiff Leslie
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 12, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANT BRADSHAW AND ASSOCIATES, P.C.'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendants Request For Production Of Documents Set Two To Defendants Special Interrogatories Set Two And To Compel Production Of Documents From Plaintiff Leslie. The court grants the motion to compel in part. Within 10 days of entry of this order, plaintiff shall (1) serve verified answers to Bradshaw's Special Interrogatories 8-14 and 17; (2) serve verified answers to Categories 15-22 and 27-29 of Bradshaw's Request for Production of Documents and to produce the documents sought by those categories, except that all attorney-client information in those documents may be redacted, other than the number of hours spent by each Berko attorney or other timekeeper on the Finley action, each attorney's or timekeeper's hourly rate, and the time increments that were billed. In all other respects, the motion is denied without prejudice to its renewal after a further meet and confer between the parties should new facts emerge entitling Bradshaw to the discovery sought. The renewed motion to be filed and served within 45 days after those new facts become known to Bradshaw. The request for sanctions is denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery matter. If the parties desire a reporter, they must meet and confer and agree upon one. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: SANDRA COLBERT VS. SUPER SHUTTLE OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiff Sandra Colbert Responses To Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Docs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 12, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANTS SUPERSHUTTLE OF SAN FRANCISCO, INC., AND SFO AIRPORTER, INC.'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff Sandra Colbert Responses To Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge Thomas Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is denied as moot because Plaintiff has served discovery responses without objection. No sanctions may be awarded in the absence of evidence itemizing the costs and expenses incurred in bringing the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BRIAN J. MCLAUGHLIN VS. 1750 DIVIS LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 12, 2015, Line 12, CROSS DEFENDANT BRIAN MCLAUGHLIN, JENNIFER MCLAUGHLIN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to all causes of action except the fifth. It appears uncontested that Brian McLaughlin's employment with 1750 Divis LLC ended in August 2009. As such, all claims related to his acts or omissions during that period of employment are barred by the relevant statutes of limitation. Overruled as to the fifth cause of action, for declaratory relief as to the ownership of 1750 Divis. The Complaint itself serves as evidence of a controversy over who owns 1750 Divis and in what proportion; declaratory relief in these circumstances is proper. The Court does not consider the purported motion to strike as the special setting in this case was only sought for the demurrers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EG)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 12, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANTS DOUG COLE, AN INDIVIDUAL, JOI SCHEELE, STAN TEETS, AND TALK FREE, INC.'S Motion For Protective Order Staying Discovery. Pro Tem Judge Thomas Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion to Stay Discovery is granted as follows: Discovery is stayed pending a ruling on Defendants' Demurrer to the First Amended Complaint. If that ruling determines that plaintiffs, or any of them, have standing, then any discovery now pending is deemed to have been served and Defendants shall have thirty days to respond unless mutually agreed otherwise. Nothing in the instant discovery ruling shall be used by either party as evidence of a substantive analysis by this court of the issues to be decided in the demurrer. No sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Sanctions are awarded in the amount of $1,560 and shall be paid by Defendant Sommers within 10 court days. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MYXOLIDIAN, LLC VS. WALTER J. CLARKE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Notices And Stay Action Or, In The Alternative, For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 12, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANTS WALTER CLARKE, JOHN ROSENFELD, AND OXFORD INVESTMENT PARTNERS, LLC'S Motion To Quash Deposition Notices And Stay Action Or, In The Alternative, For Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge Thomas Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' Motion to Stay Discovery is granted until the application for an order to arbitrate is determined. CCP section 1281.4 is mandatory. The motion to quash deposition notices or to issue a protective order is denied without prejudice. No sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
